head.title1: Il Bearč — Holiday Cottage in Feltrone-Socchieve, Carnia | Carnic Alps
head.metadesc: Il Bearč, holiday cottage in the Carnic Alps near the Friulian Dolomites — Feltrone (Socchieve), Carnia, 696 m. Fenced garden, hiking trails, sleeps up to 4 guests.
home.p1: Holiday Cottage in Feltrone · Socchieve, Carnia (UD)
home.div1: Check availability Experience the atmosphere of Il Bearč
about.h21:
A house in the heart
of the Carnic Alps
about.p2: You will find us in Feltrone, a charming little village in Carnia, surrounded by greenery at 696 metres above sea level, in the municipality of Socchieve, in the Friuli Venezia Giulia region, close to the Austrian border.
about.p3: The house enjoys a sunny position from morning to evening and has a large fenced garden where children can play — ideal for anyone seeking peace and quiet, far from noise and pollution.
about.p4: Walks through ancient beech and fir woods framed by the striking peaks of the Carnic Alps, hiking, mountain biking, skiing. Il Bearč is the perfect starting point for experiencing the authentic mountains.
servizi.h21: The comfort of an authentic stay
servizi.p2: Breathtaking views of the majestic Carnic Alps directly from the house — wake up every morning to an extraordinary landscape.
servizi.p3: Fast Wi-Fi included for your whole stay — about 68 Mbps download and 20 Mbps upload, great for remote working and streaming too. Or disconnect in total freedom.
servizi.p4: Large fenced garden and two terraces overlooking the Carnic Alps. Outdoor space that is ideal for the whole family.
servizi.p5: Free public parking right next to the house. Open-air, always available, no reservation needed.
servizi.p6: The perfect climate in every season thanks to the air-conditioning system, so you can sleep and relax in ideal conditions all year round.
servizi.p7: Complete kitchen with dishwasher, microwave, hob and toaster. Everything you need to feel at home.
servizi.p8: Flat-screen TV with satellite channels in the living room. Spacious lounge and dining area for pleasant evenings at home.
servizi.p9: Pellet stove in the living room for cold evenings. Pellets are included in the price of your stay: nothing to worry about, just the enveloping warmth of a mountain home.
servizi.p10: One bedroom with a double bed, one bedroom with two single beds. Bed linen and towels provided. A cot for small children is available on request.
attivita.p2: Italy's first experiential botanical garden, in Ampezzo in Carnia. Over 5 hectares of natural parkland with 400+ plant species and a 2 km loop trail through three different forest ecosystems. By day you explore medicinal plants and Alpine biodiversity; by night the forest turns into a unique sensory experience thanks to the illuminated trail — one of the longest of its kind in Europe. A visit scientifically proven to reduce stress and restore body and mind. Booking required → luminamilia.com
attivita.p3: The Zoncolan and Forni di Sopra ski slopes are just 30 km away. Never too crowded, perfect for relaxed skiing. There are also cross-country trails in the area.
attivita.p4: 6 routes starting directly from Feltrone: from the panoramic Col Gentile (2,075 m) to the partisan trail "Troi da l'Ors", from the loop of the Socchieve villages to Monte Corona. Something for every level, from families to expert hikers. Discover all the routes →
attivita.h34: Mountain biking and enduro — Bike Arena Ravascletto/Zoncolan
attivita.p5: About 40 minutes by car from Feltrone (27.7 km via the SR355), the new Bike Arena Ravascletto/Zoncolan has opened, dedicated to mountain biking and enduro. Three trails start from the Funifor mid-station and run down to the base of the lift: Bad Bunny (blue, 5.5 km / 315 m, smooth flow trail also suitable for beginners), Joker (red, 2.8 km / 330 m, intense pace with jumps and technical sections) and Supernatural (black, 2 km / 328 m, roots and freeride for expert riders). Day ticket with unlimited Funifor lift rides: €30 full price, €20 reduced. On site: Bike Wash area, post-ride relaxation zone and courses for adults and children. Open throughout the summer season.
attivita.p6: The Carnia valleys also offer routes for every level, with rental and assistance in nearby centres and signposted itineraries throughout the area.
attivita.p7: The woods around Feltrone are home to roe deer, red deer, chamois, squirrels, black grouse and — with a little luck — golden eagles. Walking quietly along the trails at dawn or dusk you may spot them in their natural habitat. For wildlife lovers, about 45 km away is the Lake Cornino Reserve, famous for the reintroduction of the griffon vulture in Friuli; further afield, in Cimolais, the Pianpinedo Wildlife Park offers 35 hectares of woodland with deer, ibex and chamois.
attivita.h36: Ampezzo Forest — Bosco Flobia and Bosco della Stua
attivita.p8: About 22 km from Feltrone, in the upper Val Lumiei towards Passo Pura, lies one of Carnia's most beautiful forests. Beeches, spruces and silver firs give way at altitude to larches and mountain pines. The Bosco Flobia educational trail — 3.5 km starting from the Tita Piaz mountain hut, 130 m of elevation gain, about 2 hours — crosses a forest famous for its resonance wood, used to build violins and stringed instruments. Nine information panels tell the story of the forest, and a continuous handrail makes the trail accessible to blind visitors as well.
attivita.p9: Wildlife: roe deer, chamois, black grouse and golden eagles.
attivita.p10: Carnian cuisine is humble in its ingredients and rich in flavour. Cjalsòns, ravioli with a unique filling where sweet meets savoury. Frico, crispy outside and soft inside. And a dessert that may well have been born right near here, in Tolmezzo: tiramisù. Discover Carnian cuisine →
attivita.p11: Ancient villages such as Sauris (a German-speaking linguistic island) and Sutrio (famous for wood carving), Romanesque parish churches, stone-and-timber houses with flower-filled balconies, and local gastronomy with the famous cjalsòns. Carnia tells centuries of Alpine history.
attivita.p12: Four museums tell the soul of this land, all a short drive from Feltrone. The “Michele Gortani” Carnian Museum of Popular Arts in Tolmezzo, housed in the 17th-century Palazzo Campeis, is one of Europe's most important ethnographic museums: over 4,000 objects on Carnian life, work and traditions from the 14th to the 20th century. The Geological Museum of Carnia in Ampezzo traces 450 million years of Earth's history, with Friulian fossils including extremely rare pterosaurs (free admission). In Pesariis, the village of clocks, the Museum of Pesarina Clockmaking tells three centuries of clockmaking art, with an open-air route of monumental clocks through the village streets. In Timau, the Museum “The Carnia Zone in the Great War 1915-1918” preserves artefacts, documents and the memory of the Carnian women porters, and can be combined with the open-air museums of trenches and walkways in the mountains. Opening hours vary greatly by season: we recommend checking before your visit on carniamusei.org.
attivita.p13: A few kilometres from Tolmezzo, the Terme di Arta spa rises around the historic Fonte Pudia spring, a sulphate-calcium-magnesium-sulphurous water emerging at 9°C, known since Roman times. In the “Palazzo delle Acque” you will find a large wellness area with heated thermal pools and hydromassage, saunas at different temperatures, a Turkish bath, sensory showers, a Kneipp path and a gym, plus beauty treatments and physiotherapy and rehabilitation services. Since 2023 there is also a new partially open-air pool with water features, a lazy river and a slide for children. The facility is accessible to people with reduced mobility, with a hoist and grab rails in the pool. The ideal place to recharge after a day of hiking, cycling or skiing. For up-to-date opening times and prices see the official page on PromoTurismoFVG.
storia.p2: Carnia holds centuries of history. Discover it through the places around you.
storia.p3: A few kilometres from the holiday cottage flows the Tagliamento, one of the few European rivers to have preserved its original morphology. Not channelled, not diverted, free to change course after every flood. 178 km long with a basin of almost 3,000 km², it crosses extraordinary landscapes from the Alps to the coastal plains, forming a unique ecosystem studied as a European reference for highly natural rivers. As early as 1782 the historian Niccolò Grassi described it as the river that symbolises Carnia.
storia.p4: Eight centuries ago this territory was guarded by the Castle of Feltrone, one of the twenty-three ancient castles of Carnia recorded by the historian Niccolò Grassi in 1782. Its lords were vassals of the Patriarch of Aquileia until 1351, when Patriarch Nicholas of Luxembourg — brother of Emperor Charles IV — had most of the Carnian castles razed to the ground. No visible trace of the castle remains — but walking through these places you tread the same ground they once walked.
storia.p5: The larches and firs that still cover the mountains around Socchieve have an extraordinary history: for over two centuries the forests of Carnia supplied the timber to build the ships of the Most Serene Republic of Venice. From 1581, no fewer than 47 Carnian forests were reserved for the Venetian Arsenal. An unexpected bond between this silent mountain and the city on the sea.
storia.p6: From the 16th to the 19th century, many men of Carnia were cramars: itinerant merchants who crossed the Alps on foot carrying on their backs the crassigne, a wooden chest of drawers containing spices, fabrics, medicines and haberdashery to sell in the lands of Central Europe. They returned to Carnia in spring, and according to tradition the leftover spices carried in the drawers of the crassigne were used by Carnian women to prepare the pistùm filling of cjarsòns. One of the first historical descriptions of the phenomenon appears in 1599 in the “Descrittione della Cargna” by Jacopo Valvasone di Maniago.
storia.p7: To prepare for your visit or dig deeper afterwards: 34 audio stories produced by the Fondazione Radio Magica ETS tell the history, art and curiosities of the Carnian museums. The illustration of the territory — depicting 34 of the 39 exhibition venues of the CarniaMusei network — is the work of Carnian artist Adriano Fruch. Short stories and curiosities — "Do you know what a fossil beach is?", "Do you know why Sauris butter was good, beautiful and precious?" — also accessible in Italian Sign Language. An original way to discover the area with your ears. Open the interactive map →
storia.p8: Carnia's historical photographic archive, based at Palazzo Frisacco in Tolmezzo: around 10,000 period photographs — over 5,000 of them available online — documenting life as it once was in the Carnian valleys, among landscapes, trades and faces. The catalogued collections also include one dedicated to Socchieve, the area surrounding Il Bearč. A way to see these places as they used to be. Browse the archive →
recensioni.p3: Exceptional!! Peace to find yourself again!
recensioni.p4: Mr Dario was extremely kind and gives advice on what to see and discover. The house is very comfortable — it has everything you need for an exceptional holiday. The silence was exactly what I was looking for. Peace and tranquillity. Thank you.
recensioni.p8: The apartment was very clean, well-equipped and beautiful. We received metal water bottles from the owner as a gift for hiking — a lovely touch. Communication was smooth and easy, he was very friendly and helpful. Good value for money, I can gladly recommend this apartment to anybody.
recensioni.p11: Looking better than in the pics!
recensioni.p12: Dario was a great host, always helpful and proactive. The accommodation was excellent, looking better than in the pictures!
recensioni.p16: Very welcoming and clean. We were greeted with traditional biscuits, board games for the children and a well-stocked library with guidebooks to explore the area. Beautifully kept garden and a spectacular view of the mountains. It was one of the best experiences I have ever had.
recensioni.p20: A lovely place on three levels, very well equipped, clean and spacious. Two balconies with a view of the surrounding mountains and a nice garden. A hefty collection of maps and guidebooks prepared by the host — very helpful! If you're looking for a quiet place far away from the hustle of civilization, this is the place for you.
recensioni.p24: Perfect house, spotlessly clean and tidy. Super kind owner. The property was perfect, nothing was missing. I hope to come back next year.
recensioni.div7: 🔵 Read all 53 reviews on Booking.com
disponibilita.div1: 💡 How it works: click on your arrival day, then on your departure day. Minimum stay: 3 nights. Once you have selected your dates, click "Check availability" — you will be redirected to Avaibook, which checks real availability in real time and processes your booking securely.
prenotazione.p2: To check availability or receive more information, write to us or give us a call. We will be happy to help you organise your stay at Il Bearč.
prenotazione.p3: You can also find us on

prenotazione.div2: No pets allowed Animals of any kind are not permitted inside the holiday cottage. For hygiene reasons and out of consideration for guests with allergies, pets cannot be accommodated. By proceeding with the booking you declare that you have read and accepted this condition.
prenotazione.div3: ✅ Everything included in the nightly price: bed linen, towels, pellets for the stove, utilities and final cleaning. No tourist tax, no hidden costs.
prenotazione.div4: 🔄 Free cancellation up to 5 days before the arrival date: full refund of the amount paid.
prenotazione.div5: 🚭 Smoking is not allowed inside the house. You may smoke in the garden or on the terraces.
prenotazione.div6: ♿ Accessibility: the house is on several levels and is not accessible to people with reduced mobility.
prenotazione.div8: 🔄 Availability is managed in real time by Avaibook. When you click, you will be redirected to the official booking system — no risk of overbooking.
posizione.p1: Open interactive map on OpenStreetMap
posizione.p3: We are in the heart of Carnia, in the municipality of Socchieve, a few kilometres from Tolmezzo and well connected to the region's main ski resorts.
posizione.p4: Easily reachable by car from the A23 motorway (Udine–Tarvisio), Carnia exit. About 55 km from Udine, about 464 km from Vienna. The nearest railway station is Carnia Station, about 25 km away.
posizione.div1: 🚗 55km from Udine ⛷️ 30km from Zoncolan 🚆 25km from Carnia Station ✈️ 100km from Trieste ✈️ 180km from Venice ✈️ 175km from Treviso ✈️ 183km from Ljubljana 🇦🇹 464km from Vienna 🏔️ 696m a.s.l. 🌲 Carnic Alps
posizione.div2: 🗺️ Open the route
posizione.h31: Everything you need within a few kilometres
posizione.p6: Feltrone is a small, quiet village. Essential services are an easy drive away in the nearby villages:
posizione.div3: 🍞 Café and bakery in Mediis 💊 Pharmacy in Mediis 💳 ATM in Ampezzo ⛽ Petrol station in Ampezzo 🛒 Supermarkets in nearby villages 🏥 A&E in Tolmezzo
posizione.p7: 📶 Connectivity: excellent mobile signal coverage and home Wi-Fi with real measured speeds of ~68 Mbps download and ~20 Mbps upload — suitable for remote working and video calls too.
faq.p2: A collection of the most common questions about Casa Vacanze Il Bearč: check-in, minimum stay, pets, payment and local services.
faq.div1: Casa Vacanze Il Bearč is in Feltrone, a hamlet of the municipality of Socchieve (Udine), in the heart of Carnia, at about 696 metres above sea level and a few kilometres from Tolmezzo.
faq.summary2: How many guests can it host and what is the house like?
faq.div2: Il Bearč is a detached holiday cottage on several levels with 2 bedrooms, 1 bathroom and room for up to 4 guests. It has a living room with satellite TV, a kitchen equipped with dishwasher and washing machine, and a large fenced garden with views of the Carnic Alps. A table and four chairs are available in the garden.
faq.div3: The house has two bedrooms: one with a double bed and one with two single beds, comfortably sleeping up to 4 people. Bed linen and towels are provided by the host and ready on your arrival.
faq.summary4: What is included in the price of the stay?
faq.div4: Everything is included in the nightly price: bed linen, towels, pellets for the stove, utilities (electricity, water, gas, heating), Wi-Fi and final cleaning. There is no tourist tax, no hidden costs and no deposit to pay.
faq.div5: Cancellation is free up to 5 days before the arrival date: in that case the full amount paid is refunded. For later cancellations, please contact the owner directly.
faq.div6: The minimum stay at Casa Vacanze Il Bearč is 3 nights in all seasons.
faq.summary7: How does check-in work and what time can I arrive?
faq.div7: Check-in is from 5:00 pm with self check-in via a key box, so you can arrive independently with no time constraints. Check-out is by 10:00 am.
faq.summary8: How is the house heated in winter?
faq.div8: The house is heated by a pellet stove in the living room. The pellets needed for your stay are included in the price: you don't have to buy or carry them. Air conditioning is available in summer.
faq.summary9: Is it suitable for families with small children?
faq.div9: Yes, Il Bearč is particularly suitable for families: the fenced garden lets children play safely. A cot for babies or very small children is available on request — just mention it when booking.
faq.summary10: Is the house accessible to people with reduced mobility?
faq.div10: No. The house is on several levels with internal stairs and is not accessible to people with motor disabilities or reduced mobility.
faq.div11: Smoking is not allowed inside the house. You may smoke outside, in the garden or on the terraces.
faq.div12: No, animals are not allowed inside the Il Bearč holiday cottage. For hygiene reasons and out of consideration for guests with allergies, pets cannot be accommodated.
faq.summary13: How do I get there by public transport?
faq.div13: The nearest railway station is Carnia Station, on the Udine–Tarvisio line. From there you can reach Feltrone by car or local connections. The nearest airports are Trieste, Venice (Marco Polo), Treviso and Ljubljana — all reachable with a hire car. The most convenient way to get here is still by car, especially for getting around the area.
faq.div14: A café and bakery can be found in Mediis, which also has the pharmacy. For an ATM and petrol station, head to Ampezzo. Supermarkets are a short drive away in the nearby villages. The nearest A&E is in Tolmezzo.
faq.summary15: How do booking and payment work?
faq.div15: You can check availability and book directly from the calendar on the website: availability is managed in real time by Avaibook, which processes payment securely. Alternatively, you can contact the owner directly by phone or email with a booking request.
ricarica-ev.h21: Arriving by electric car is even easier
ricarica-ev.p2: If you travel by electric car, Il Bearč welcomes you worry-free: along the way and around the holiday cottage there are several public charging stations, ideal for planning a relaxed stop before reaching Feltrone.
ricarica-ev.p3: Use the tool below to find the most convenient charging stations for your journey in real time: location, power and availability always up to date.
ricarica-ev.div1: Find the charging station nearest to you
ricarica-ev.li1: Public charging stations along the main routes to Carnia.
ricarica-ev.li2: Charging stations around Tolmezzo, Socchieve and neighbouring villages.
ricarica-ev.li3: Plan your journey with just one or two targeted stops.
ricarica-ev.p4: Charging station information may change over time. We therefore always recommend checking up-to-date data directly on the platform indicated, before departure and during your journey.
